key_topics: Number: standard form (scientific notation), bounds and error intervals
Surds and irrational numbers (introduction)
Algebra: quadratic expressions — expanding, factorising simple quadratics
Solving quadratic equations by factorisation
Simultaneous equations: graphical and algebraic methods
Inequalities: representing and solving on a number line
Graphs: quadratic graphs, real-life graphs, distance-time graphs
Trigonometry: sin, cos, tan in right-angled triangles
Geometry: circle theorems (angles subtended at centre and circumference)
Vectors: basic vector notation, adding and subtracting vectors
Transformations: combined transformations
Statistics: cumulative frequency, box plots, histograms
Probability: conditional probability, Venn diagrams
competences: Apply trigonometric ratios to find missing sides and angles
Solve simultaneous equations by substitution and elimination
Factorise and solve simple quadratic equations
Construct and interpret cumulative frequency graphs and box plots
typical_mistakes: Using sin, cos, tan with the wrong sides (not identifying O, A, H correctly)
Sign errors when solving simultaneous equations by elimination
Forgetting to reverse inequality sign when multiplying/dividing by negative
Errors reading cumulative frequency (reading wrong axis)
Confusing dependent and independent events in probability

key_topics: Reading: analytical response to literary and non-literary texts
Understanding context: historical, social, cultural influences on texts
Close reading: effect of individual word and phrase choices
Unseen poetry: analysing form, structure, and language
Writing: extended analytical essay (multi-paragraph)
Creative writing: controlled original composition (prose or poetry)
Argument and rhetoric: evaluating the effectiveness of arguments
Research-based writing with full citations
Grammar: syntactic complexity, register, style
Editing: peer and self-editing for precision and effect
Speaking: formal presentation with Q&A, collaborative debate
competences: Write sophisticated multi-paragraph analytical essays with embedded quotations
Respond to unseen poetry with technical analysis
Produce original creative writing with controlled voice and style
Evaluate and challenge arguments in spoken and written form
typical_mistakes: Retelling plot instead of analysing technique
Poetry analysis that only describes rather than evaluates effect

key_topics: Biology: genetics — DNA, chromosomes, genes, heredity
Biology: variation, natural selection, evolution
Biology: health — disease, immune system, vaccines
Chemistry: the Periodic Table — groups, periods, trends
Chemistry: chemical bonding — ionic, covalent, metallic (intro)
Chemistry: rates of reaction — factors affecting speed of reaction
Chemistry: acids and bases — pH, neutralisation, salts
Physics: electromagnetism — electric and magnetic fields
Physics: electromagnetic induction — generators and transformers (intro)
Physics: nuclear physics — atomic structure, radioactivity, fission/fusion
competences: Explain heredity using DNA, genes, and chromosomes
Describe trends across the Periodic Table
Analyse factors affecting reaction rates with experimental evidence
Explain electromagnetic induction and its applications
typical_mistakes: Confusing DNA, gene, and chromosome (hierarchy not understood)
Thinking groups go across the Periodic Table (not periods)
Thinking alpha radiation is most penetrating (it's least)
